message:
feat(core,mvcc,pager): shared conflict observer, always-on column defaults, type-affinity JOIN keys, page1 merge fix

Five interconnected engine improvements spanning the core connection,
MVCC transaction manager, and physical merge ladder:

1. Shared conflict observer (Arc<MetricsObserver>):
   The per-connection MetricsObserver was previously Rc-wrapped and
   local to each Connection, meaning conflict stats (page contentions,
   FCW drifts, SSI aborts) were invisible across connections sharing the
   same database path. Promoted to Arc and stored on SharedMvccState so
   all connections sharing a database file report into the same observer.
   The InProcessPageLockTable now receives the observer via
   with_observer() constructor. New PRAGMA fsqlite.conflict_stats rows
   expose fcw_merge_attempts, fcw_merge_successes, and top_hotspots.

2. Always-on column defaults:
   Column default evaluation was previously skipped for programs without
   INSERT opcodes. This broke SELECT on rows predating ALTER TABLE ADD
   COLUMN — those rows have fewer columns than the current schema and
   the VDBE engine needs the column defaults map to fill missing values.
   Now always populates runtime_inputs with defaults (fast short-circuit
   when no defaults exist in the schema).

3. Type-affinity coercion in HashableJoinKey:
   Hash-join keys now normalize TEXT values that represent valid numbers
   to their numeric form before hashing and comparison, matching
   SQLite's implicit type-affinity semantics in JOIN ON conditions.
   Integer(10) and Text("10") now hash and compare equally. Includes
   proper Float-to-Integer normalization for exact-integer floats to
   maintain hash consistency.

4. Removed MemDatabase fast-path for in-memory scans:
   The :memory: database optimization that read directly from MemDatabase
   was removed because all writes now go through the VDBE/pager path.
   Both file-backed and :memory: databases now consistently scan through
   the normal query path reading from pager-backed state.

5. Page1 header preservation in physical merge:
   Level-3 merge ladder (cell-level merge) now copies the first 100
   bytes of the committed page back onto the merged result when
   operating on page 1, preserving the SQLite file header which
   contains the schema cookie, page count, and other metadata that
   must not be overwritten by the base page's stale header.

6. NoConflict merge ladder result:
   Transaction lifecycle now handles MergeLadderResult::NoConflict by
   recording the page read at latest_seq and updating write_set_versions,
   allowing transactions to succeed when the base is physically unchanged.

Feature flags: Added linux-asupersync-uring feature to fsqlite-mvcc and
fsqlite-pager Cargo.toml files, threading through to fsqlite-vfs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@ rust-version.workspace = true
 default = ["native"]
 native = ["fsqlite-pager/native", "fsqlite-wal/native"]
 wasm = ["fsqlite-pager/wasm", "fsqlite-wal/wasm"]
+linux-asupersync-uring = ["fsqlite-pager/linux-asupersync-uring"]
 
 [dependencies]
 blake3 = { workspace = true }

@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@ rust-version.workspace = true
 default = ["native"]
 native = ["fsqlite-types/native", "fsqlite-vfs/native"]
 wasm = ["fsqlite-types/wasm", "fsqlite-vfs/wasm"]
+linux-asupersync-uring = ["fsqlite-vfs/linux-asupersync-uring"]
 
 [dependencies]
 foldhash = "0.1"
